title: How to Create an Effective Startup Roadmap
author: Amit Sharma
content:
1. Start with the Problem

Identify a real, painful problem. Ask: 'Is this a must-have or just nice to have?'

  • Talk to real users.
  • Validate the audience size.

title: Defining Your Vision and Mission
author: Amit Sharma
content:
Vision vs. Mission

Your Vision is the long-term impact. Your Mission is the method to get there.

This provides your roadmap with a clear North Star.

title: The Art of Filtering Ideas
author: Amit Sharma
content:
How to evaluate solutions:

  1. Feasibility: Can we build it?
  2. Market Demand: Do they want it?
  3. Revenue: Can we monetize it?

title: Building Your First MVP
author: Amit Sharma
content:
Strip it down

Build only what is necessary to solve the problem. Focus on value, not features.

Example: For a job portal, start with just registration and job posting.

title: Phase 1: Research & Validation
author: Amit Sharma
content:
The Foundation

Before coding, perform:

  • Competitor analysis
  • User interviews
  • Market surveys

title: Phase 2: MVP Development & Internal Testing
author: Amit Sharma
content:
Execution

Focus on the core loop of your product. Perform internal testing (Alpha) to catch major bugs before users see it.

title: Phase 3: Launch and Feedback Loops
author: Amit Sharma
content:
Soft Launch

Release to a small group of early adopters. Use tools like Hotjar or Mixpanel to see how they behave.

title: Scaling Your Startup Team
author: Amit Sharma
content:
Growth Phase

When you have product-market fit, it is time to hire. Focus on multi-talented individuals who can handle the chaos of a startup.

title: The Power of Pivoting
author: Amit Sharma
content:
Stay Flexible

A roadmap is a guide, not a rulebook. If the data says your idea isn't working, pivot to a new direction based on user feedback.
